Correct answer: A

Q2 MSQ Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +1 / -0
Not attempted

The number 12375 is divisible by

Correct answer: A, B, C
Solution

(a, b, c)
• For a number to be divisible by 3 and 9, the sum of its digits should be divisible by 3 and 9
respectively. Here, 1 + 2 + 3 + 7 + 5 = 18, which is divisible 3 and 9 both. Hence, the number is
divisible by both 3 and 9.
• For a number to be divisible by 11, the difference between the sum of digits in the odd
positions and even positions must be a multiple of 11 or 0. Here, (5 + 3 + 1) – (7 + 2) = 0. Hence,
the number is divisible by 11.

Q5 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Sum of all numbers n such that n! ends with 30 zeros.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
124! ends with 28 zeros and 125! ends with 31 zeros.
Therefore, there is no integer n with n! ending in exactly 30 zeros.

Q9 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • COMPUTER SCIENCE • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Read the passage carefully and choose the best answer to the following question from the four alternatives given below.
Among the natural resources which can be called upon in national plan for development, possibly
the most important is human labour; without productive labour force, including effective leadership
and intelligent middle management, no amount of foreign assistance or natural wealth can ensure
successful development and modernisation. One essential factor is usually overlooked or ignored.
The forgotten factor is the role of women. Development will be handicapped as long as women
remain second-class citizens, uneducated, without any voice in family or community decisions,
without legal or economic status, married when they are still practically children, and thenceforth
producing one baby after another, often only to see half of them die before they are of school age.
We can enhance development by improving ‘woman power’ by giving women opportunity to develop themselves.
The most important natural resource for national development is

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)
The passage clearly states that among natural resources, human labour is the most important for national development.

He likes to drive his car at a speed of eighty kilometres each hour.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

grammatically incorrect in this context. “Correct sentence: “He likes to drive his car at a speed of
eighty kilometres an hour.”

Q17 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Consider the following statements:
S1: With register window overlap, the need for memory access in parameter passing between procedures is minimized.
S2: Overlapping register windows reduce the frequency of saving and restoring registers to memory during procedure calls.
Which of the above statements is correct?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• Overlapping windows share registers between caller and callee. This allows procedure arguments to be passed directly in registers, avoiding extra memory loads/stores. So, S1 is correct.
• With register windows, most procedure calls do not need to save registers to memory. The hardware just switches to a new window, reducing context save/restore overhead. So, S2 is correct.

Q37 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements is/are correct?

Correct answer: B, C
Solution

(b, c)
• 2PL guarantees conflict-serializability regardless of the order in which locks are acquired, as
long as the protocol (growing then shrinking phase) is followed. So, option (a) is incorrect.
• If a transaction releases locks before committing, other transactions may read uncommitted
values. If the first transaction aborts, the dependent transactions must also abort this is called
cascading aborts. So, option (b) is correct.
• Even though 2PL ensures serializability, a transaction may wait indefinitely for locks if other
transactions continuously acquire them, causing starvation. So, option (c) is correct.

3. IR [Address] → MAR(take the value stored in instruction register and move into MAR).

8. ACC ← MBR (Move the value stored at MBR to accumulator).

Q44 MCQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Let X and Y be two sets having n elements each. Then the total number of Bijective functions from X and Y is

Correct answer: B
Solution

(a)
Bijection means both Onto and One-One. Now, if we have two sets with X and Y with N elements
each, each element from Y has to map exactly 1 element of X.
So, this simplifies your problem to place N different balls to N different boxes, where each box
can hold only 1 ball. In other way, you just have to find out how many ways you can arrange the
N elements of Y.
Thus, N!.

Solution

twice one for each table.

Q55 MSQ Not checked
Technical • COMPUTER SCIENCE & IT • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A full subtractor is possible to realise

Correct answer: B, C
Solution

(b, c)
Full subtractor is having 3 input variables and two outputs.
To realise full subtractor two 8 × 1 multiplexers are required, no need of NOT gate.
To realise full subtractor two 4 × 1 multiplexers and one NOT gate is required.
To realise full subtractor two 3 × 8 time decoder and two OR gates are required.

side.
